title: mascagnia surinamensis
intro: Mascagnia surinamensis, also known as Suriname horse-ear or Mascagnia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Mascagnia surinamensis include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Mascagnia surinamensis can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate various health conditions.
